name: Hominy-Squash Stew
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Light olive oil (and sunflower seed oil)
1 lb Fresh or canned tomatoes (peeled, seeded and chopped; juice reserved)
3 cups Peeled, cubed banana squash (in 1-inch cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the soaked beans, covering them generously with fresh water and bring to a boil. Boil vigorously about 5 minutes, skim off any foam that rises to the surface, then lower the heat and simmer for 30 minutes. Add half a teaspoon of salt and continue cooking until the beans are tender, about another 30 minutes. Taste as they cook to ensure they are done as desired. Drain the beans but reserve the liquid.
2. Warm a small, heavy skillet and toast the cumin seeds until fragrant. Add the oregano, stirring for about 5 seconds, then quickly remove them to a plate so they don't burn. Combine with the cinnamon stick and cloves and grind to a powder in an electric spice mill.
3. Heat oil in a wide skillet and sauté the onion briskly over high heat for 1 minute. Lower the heat to medium and add the garlic, ground spice mixture, ground red chile, and salt to taste. Stir to combine.
4. Add half the reserved bean broth and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions soften. Add the tomatoes, tomato juice, squash, and enough bean broth to cover. Simmer for about 20 minutes until the squash is partially cooked.
5. Stir in the hominy, beans, and diced chiles. Add more broth as needed and continue cooking until the squash is tender. Taste and check seasonings. Serve garnished with cilantro and sour cream or shredded cheese.
